thesis: Catalyst: Process gate—rebalance not permitted today (weekly spacing; rebalance due today=false). Preserve signal integrity; defer any rotation until we can re-score with tool-verified WEEKLY 30/90d momentum plus (equities) ratios/news on the next eligible date.

Factor edge: Turnover avoidance is the edge today—without verifiable price/return inputs (today’s tool price data is unavailable), any change would be unscored and increases model risk/slippage risk in a 30-day horizon framework.

Variant view: If the tape has shifted materially in the last 24–72h, we will not react without verification; maintaining diversified exposures (growth + health care + energy + gold + payments) avoids accidental factor bets based on unverified data.
risks: Macro: If an unobserved (today) risk-off shock occurred, growth-heavy exposure (NVDA/META/AVGO/AMZN) could draw down before the next eligible rebalance; cannot quantify without verified prices.
Idiosyncratic: Earnings/guidance/regulatory headlines are UNKNOWN today (no verified news pull; and price tools failed), so single-name gap risk may be present but unmeasured.
Execution/liquidity: Deferring changes concentrates potential turnover into the next eligible rebalance window; mitigate then via staged resizing and limiting changes to the highest score deltas.
falsifiable_checks: Next eligible rebalance (>=7 days cadence): require tool-verified WEEKLY history for all holdings from lookback_30d_start=2026-01-24 and lookback_90d_start=2025-11-25 plus SPY/QQQ; if any holding lacks weekly history, do not add risk and prefer simplification only.

At next eligible rebalance close: if any holding’s tool-verified 90d return underperforms SPY by >=10.0% (weekly series) AND has negative 30d return, escalate it to top replacement review; otherwise keep as core unless news shows thesis damage.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: Not a rebalance day and today’s verified pricing inputs are unavailable (prices/returns UNKNOWN), so expected edge cannot be validated and does not clear trading-cost/turnover drag. Bull: If the prior portfolio thesis remains intact, doing nothing preserves exposure to ongoing quality+momentum winners. Bear: If regime has shifted, we accept short-term tracking risk rather than taking unverified action; next eligible rebalance is the correct checkpoint to re-risk with validated data.
